replication_study.hypothesis: Among Asian-American participants, making their ethnic identity salient (givenprimingquestionnaire = 1) lowers their reservation weekly discount rate. Operationalised as a negative coefficient on the treatment dummy in an interval (or midpoint) regression of the log lower- and upper-bound interest rate (lndiscratelo / lndiscratehi) on the treatment and standard controls (largestakes, longterm, largelong).

data_plan.wave_or_subset: Round-2 (hence the file suffix R2); only Asian subjects (asian==1) are analysed.
data_plan.sample_size: 745 unique subjects; 2,980 observations in long format (stakes × horizon).
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Subject × (stakes × horizon) cell; clustering at subject id.
data_plan.access_details: Provided directly in replication_data (unrestricted, local path /app/data/replication_data).

qualification.explanation: Round-2 constitutes a fresh, independent sample collected with the **same protocol** as Round-1 used for the original claim, allowing a high-fidelity but independent test of robustness.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Variables, tasks, and coding match the originals described in the paper and in ReadMe.pdf (pp.1-4) – e.g., lndiscratelo / lndiscratehi, givenprimingquestionnaire, largestakes, longterm, largelong, asian dummy. These are exactly the variables used in the Stata code that generated the original coefficient (see RepTable2.do lines 19-23).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original claim relied on the first collection (Round-1) indicated in initial_details.txt and post_registration.json; the replication uses the file with suffix R2 which contains later experimental sessions not included in the original analysis (confirmed by its larger N = 745 vs. 71 Asians in original). Thus, it is a new sample drawn under identical conditions.

data_plan.notes: Dataset already contains lower and upper log-rate bounds. A few upper bounds are missing (lndiscratehi NaN), rows with missing midpoints will be dropped list-wise. The long format (stakes, term) implies 4 rows per participant; clustering by id addresses non-independence.

planned_method.steps:
1. Load REPExperiment1DataR2.dta from /app/data/replication_data.
2. Restrict to asian == 1.
3. Construct mid_lograte = (lndiscratelo + lndiscratehi)/2 as point estimate of interval centre (approximation to interval regression).
4. Regress mid_lograte on givenprimingquestionnaire, largestakes, longterm, largelong with a constant.
5. Use robust standard errors clustered at the subject id level.
6. Extract coefficient and p-value for givenprimingquestionnaire.
7. Save summary to /app/data/replication_asian_discount_results.txt.
planned_method.models: Ordinary Least Squares on midpoint with cluster-robust SEs (approximation to interval regression used in original Stata intreg).

planned_estimation_and_test.test: Two-sided t-test with cluster-robust variance (groups = id).

planned_method.missing_data_handling: List-wise deletion where either bound is missing (statsmodels default with missing='drop').
planned_method.inference_criteria: Evidence will be considered supportive if the coefficient is negative and p < 0.05 (two-tailed); consistency in sign with original (negative) is required.

files.replication_data/replicate_experiment1_a: Loads the Round-2 Experiment-1 data, filters Asian subjects, computes midpoints of discount-rate bounds, fits cluster-robust OLS, prints and saves the regression summary.

codebase.notes: Script paths use the canonical mount /app/data/... All I/O occurs inside this directory so the container policy is satisfied.

analysis.comparison_metrics: 1) Sign of treatment coefficient; 2) Magnitude relative to original −1.4165; 3) p-value significance (<0.05). These will be compared descriptively to the original coefficient and p-value reported in initial_details.txt.
